macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Tech Lifts U.S. Stock Futures Ahead of South Korean Memory Giants' AI Earnings Test
items.summary: U.S. stock futures rose on Monday as investors prepare for Samsung's earnings release on Tuesday and SK Hynix's upcoming massive U.S. listing, with both South Korean memory giants set to test the artificial-intelligence trade.

items.title: Japan's Yen Hits Weakest Level Against U.S. Dollar, Raising Cost of Living Concerns
items.summary: Japan's currency dropped to its weakest level against the U.S. dollar, making imported goods, fuel, and food more expensive and increasing living costs for households and businesses.

items.title: OPEC+ Members Agree to Modest Oil Production Increase Amid Fluctuating Crude Prices
items.summary: Major OPEC+ members agreed to a modest increase in collective oil production quotas for next month, while crude prices fluctuated after two days of gains amid resilient flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: SK Hynix Reduces ADR Deal Size to $28 Billion, Slightly Shaking Tech Trade Sentiments
items.summary: SK Hynix reduced its ADR deal size from $29 billion to $28 billion, a marginal change that may shake market sentiments around the tech trade as the company prepares for its U.S. listing.

items.title: Easyjet Shares Surge on $5 Billion Takeover Offer from U.S. Firm Castle Lake
items.summary: Shares of UK budget carrier Easyjet soared after agreeing in principle to a takeover offer from U.S. firm Castle Lake, worth more than £5 billion.

items.title: Central Bank Interest Rate Forecasts Shift Higher Through 2028 Amid Trump-Iran Conflict
items.summary: Forecasts for central bank borrowing costs have shifted higher, with trajectories elevated by at least half a percentage point through 2028, largely due to President Trump's war against Iran.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Rallies Above $63,500 as ETF Inflows End 10-Day Selling Streak
items.summary: Bitcoin surged to $63,548 following weak US jobs data, with Bitcoin ETFs recording a $221 million inflow that ended a painful 10-day outflow streak, signaling renewed institutional interest.

items.title: Ondo Finance Launches SEC-Aligned Tokenized Stock Model with BlackRock ETF
items.summary: Ondo Finance debuted blockchain-based versions of BlackRock's iShares Core S&P 500 ETF and Micron Technology shares, structured to operate within the existing US securities system under the SEC's staff statement for tokenized securities.

items.title: Ethereum Holds Above $1,750 After 12% Rally Amid Bullish Momentum
items.summary: Ethereum price stabilized above $1,750 following a 12% weekly rally, as bulls battle key resistance levels near $1,800 while market sentiment improves on weak jobs data.

items.title: Revolut Drops Tether's USDT in Europe, Boosting Circle's USDC
items.summary: Revolut announced it will remove Tether's USDT from its European platform, handing the regulated market to Circle's USDC amid increasing MiCA compliance pressures.

items.title: Ripple Unlocks 1 Billion XRP Tokens on Schedule Despite Bearish Pressure
items.summary: Ripple proceeded with its scheduled unlock of 1 billion XRP tokens, even as the asset faces bearish market pressure and ongoing ETF demand speculation for a potential H2 price bounce.

items.title: Shiba Inu Surpasses 1.6 Million Holders as 7.64 Million SHIB Burned
items.summary: Shiba Inu reached over 1.6 million holders while investors accumulated 51 billion SHIB from exchanges, with the project's deflation mechanism burning 7.64 million tokens in recent hours.

items.title: JPMorgan Warns Strategy's $1.25B Bitcoin Monetization Plan Increases BTC Risk
items.summary: JPMorgan analysts cautioned that MicroStrategy's new $1.25 billion Bitcoin monetization plan could elevate risk exposure for Bitcoin holders, citing potential market volatility implications.

items.title: Vitalik Buterin Shares Top Priorities for New 'Lean Ethereum' Strawmap
items.summary: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin outlined key priorities for his new 'Lean Ethereum' strawmap, focusing on scalability and efficiency improvements for the network.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google DeepMind Leaks Gemini 3.5 Pro, Targeting July 17 Launch to Rival Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6
items.summary: New leaks reveal Google DeepMind is preparing Gemini 3.5 Pro, its strongest AI model yet, with a broader launch expected on July 17 after pre-training refinements; the model aims to compete directly with Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 in long-horizon reasoning, coding, and multimodal intelligence.

items.title: Chinese Startup Zidai Releases Open-Weight GLM 5.2, Approaching Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT 5.5 in Coding Benchmarks
items.summary: Zidai's GLM 5.2 is now available as an open-weight model that users can download and run, demonstrating performance near Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT 5.5 on coding and agentic benchmarks.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Science, a Research App Built on $400M Biotech Acquisition to Counter OpenAI
items.summary: Anthropic has launched Claude Science, a research application built on a $400 million biotech acquisition and the hiring of John Jumper, positioning it as a direct competitor to OpenAI's AI research initiatives.

items.title: OpenAI Partners with Broadcom on Custom 'Jalapeno' Inference Chip for Better Performance Per Watt
items.summary: OpenAI has teamed up with Broadcom to develop a custom inference chip named 'Jalapeno,' claiming superior performance per watt compared to competing hardware solutions.

items.title: US Government Lifts Export Curbs, Bringing Anthropic's Advanced AI Models Back Online
items.summary: After the US government lifted strict export curbs, Anthropic's advanced AI models have returned online, marking a significant regulatory shift in AI model availability.
items.source: Instagram (Anthropic's advanced AI models are back online)

items.title: AI Engineering Job Listings at Big Companies Grew 50–100% Year Over Year Despite Entry-Level Shrinking
items.summary: While entry-level software openings shrank, AI engineering job listings at major companies surged 50–100% year over year, with professionals possessing multiple AI skills earning over 40% more than those without.
items.source: YouTube (Is Becoming a Software Engineer Still Worth It in 2026?)

items.title: Chrome Extension Enables Hand-Drawn Video Call Interactions Using Only Your Hand
items.summary: A new Chrome extension allows users to draw, point, and circle on video calls using only hand gestures, adding a new superpower to virtual communication.
items.source: Instagram (Video calls just got a new superpower)

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Rockstar Reportedly Distancing Itself from Sony's GTA 6 Branding
items.summary: Rockstar Games is reportedly shifting away from Sony's specific branding for the upcoming Grand Theft Auto 6, signaling a potential change in the game's marketing strategy ahead of its release.

items.title: Robert Bowling Launches '18 Bravo' Studio with Bold Open-Source Promise
items.summary: Former Call of Duty strategist Robert Bowling has founded a new independent studio called 18 Bravo, pledging to open-source any commercially failed game to ensure community preservation rather than letting it disappear.

items.title: Sony Announces End of Physical Game Discs by 2028
items.summary: Sony officially stated that starting January 2028, all new PlayStation titles will be digital-only, eliminating discs, physical copies, and used game markets for the platform.

items.title: Pokemon GO Announces Community Day Voting for August 2025
items.summary: Pokemon GO has brought back Community Day voting for its upcoming August 2025 event, offering fans four Pokemon candidates to choose as the spotlight character.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Tesla Expands Robotaxi Service to Miami
items.summary: Tesla has made its robotaxi service available in a small section of Miami, marking an expansion of its autonomous ride-hailing operations.

items.title: Tesla Model Y Long Wheelbase Launches in US with Six Seats
items.summary: Tesla has started taking orders for the Model Y Long Wheelbase in the US and Puerto Rico, featuring six seats and a price tag of $62,000.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold Debuts as Most Advanced Foldable
items.summary: Samsung unveiled the Galaxy Z TriFold, its most advanced foldable yet, featuring a redesigned dual-hinge system, upgraded materials, and a 10-inch display.

items.title: Form Energy Secures $405M for 100-Hour Battery Development
items.summary: Form Energy, a company producing longer-lasting alternatives to lithium batteries, secured $405 million in funding to accelerate development of its 100-hour battery.

items.title: Elon Musk's X Returns to Brazil After Suspension
items.summary: The social media platform X is returning to Brazil after being inaccessible for over a month, having complied with all judicial demands following a clash with a Brazilian justice.

items.title: American Water Cyberattack Highlights Critical Infrastructure Risks
items.summary: A continuing cyberattack on the nation's largest regulated water utility has renewed focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure.

items.title: Sony Converts EU PlayStation Disc Factory to Optical Microlenses Plant
items.summary: Sony has invested $34 million to repurpose its EU PlayStation disc factory into a plant for manufacturing optical microlenses.

items.title: Space Startups See Funding Growth Amid Capex-Intensive Ventures
items.summary: A report indicates that space startups are experiencing funding growth as investors bet on capital-intensive ventures.

items.title: Northvolt Head of Main Plant to Step Down
items.summary: Battery maker Northvolt announced that the head of its main plant will step down from the position.

items.title: Spotify Starts Free Streaming Option in South Korea
items.summary: Spotify has launched a free streaming option for users in South Korea.
